brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +48.4% since Feb 27 — Brent $105.33 hero-stat (Friday Apr 24 cash close; weekend — no new settle); Trading Economics CFD Apr 24: $104.63 (-0.42% d/d); intraday range Apr 24 ~$104.63–$106.01 — Weekly: Brent ~+14%, WTI ~+17% — Indefinite ceasefire D4; Araghchi delivers demands to Pakistan; IKIA flights resume — EIA Apr 24: dated Brent spot premium >$25/bbl over front-month futures
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 17 settle: $90.38/bbl (-9.0%, intraday low ~$86.84) — five-week lows per Trading Economics; sharp sell-off on Iran's mid-afternoon Hormuz 'fully open' announcement before partial recovery into settle. APR 17-18 HORMUZ WHIPSAW: FM Araghchi declared strait 'fully open' Apr 17 via coordinated Qeshm-Larak routing for duration of Lebanon ceasefire — only ~9 ships transited per CNN — before IRGC Navy REVERSED Apr 18 declaring strait closed until US lifts blockade: 'approaching the Strait of Hormuz will be considered cooperation with the enemy, and any offending vessel will be targeted.' Iranian gunboats fired on a tanker Apr 18; unknown projectile hit container vessel; two India-flagged vessels struck (India summoned Iran's ambassador). IMF PortWatch: 14 vessels transiting in prior 24 hours (mostly Iranian-flagged) — ~95% below pre-war baseline. Polymarket: 78.5% probability Hormuz does NOT normalize by April 30. Trump (Apr 18 aboard AF1): 'maybe I won't extend it, so you have a blockade, and unfortunately we have to start dropping bombs again'; warned he would secure Iran's enriched uranium 'in a much more unfriendly form' absent deal; said Iran got 'cute' trying to reopen Hormuz with fees. IEA April OMR (Apr 14-15): global oil supply fell 10.1 mb/d in March to 97 mb/d — largest disruption on record; Hormuz shipments 20+ mb/d pre-war → 3.8 mb/d early April; net export loss >13 mb/d; OECD spare capacity fell to 320 kb/d (lowest on record); North Sea dated crude ~$130/bbl at report time. Bank forecasts: Goldman cut Q2 Brent $90 (severe case $120 Q3 if Hormuz stays closed another month); JPMorgan $100 Q2 / $90 Q3 / $80 Q4; Macquarie: upside to $200 if war extends past June. EIA STEO (Apr 7): Brent peaks $115/bbl Q2 before easing to $88 by Q4. Treasury (Bessent) extended through May 16 waiver on sanctioned Russian crude loaded before cutoff — explicitly to cushion oil prices. OPEC+ voluntary +206 kb/d May bump physically blocked by Hormuz; next ministerial May 3. Source: ICE Futures Europe / CNBC / Bloomberg / IEA / EIA / Goldman / JPMorgan / CNN / WSJ / Polymarket.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +41.9% since Feb 27 — WTI $94.40/bbl (Friday Apr 24 cash close; weekend — no new settle) — Brent $105.33 — gas $4.022 (AAA Apr 24, flat) — UMich final April Consumer Sentiment 49.8 (LOWEST ON RECORD) — S&P 7,165.08 RECORD CLOSE held into weekend; Nasdaq 24,836.60 RECORD CLOSE — Day 57: Iran ceasefire D18 holds; IKIA international flights resume
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 17 settle: $83.85/bbl (-12.0%, intraday low ~$79.78) per CNBC/CME/NYMEX — sharpest single-session move since Apr 8 on Iran's mid-afternoon claim Hormuz was 'fully open' (reversed ~18 hours later). Markets closed Saturday Apr 18 and Sunday Apr 19. AAA national average gas: $4.09/gal Apr 16 (-7¢ W/W); drifting to $4.076 on current state-averages page; prior weeks $4.16 (Apr 9), $4.08 (Apr 2). EIA STEO (Apr 7) projected retail gasoline peak $4.30 / diesel >$5.80 during April. March CPI (Apr 10) +3.3% YoY (highest since Apr 2024) / +0.9% MoM; core 2.6%; gas component +21.2% MoM — largest monthly jump since CPI gasoline series began 1967; airline fares +2.7% MoM. April CPI scheduled May 12. UMich preliminary April consumer sentiment 47.6 — ALL-TIME LOW in 74-year series (below June 2022 pandemic floor of 50), -10.7% MoM; 1-yr inflation expectations 4.8% (sharpest jump since Apr 2025); 5-10 yr 3.4%. 98% of responses collected BEFORE Apr 7 ceasefire — April final reading Apr 24 key signal. EIA weekly (week ending Apr 10): commercial crude inventories -913k bbl to 463.8M; Cushing -1.7M (largest since Jan 2025); refinery utilization 89.6%. SPR: DOE running coordinated 172M bbl release as part of IEA 400M bbl global action; initial awards 45.2M bbl. War-risk insurance 0.3-1.0%+ per transit (peaks >5% on crowd-sourced trackers); pre-war 0.05-0.15%. DFC backstopping Hormuz war-risk cover. Fed funds 3.50-3.75%; Fed Funds futures repriced aggressively toward December 2026 cut. Source: CNBC / NYMEX / AAA / BLS / EIA / DOE / UMich / Treasury / Federal Reserve.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.042 / +35.0% since Feb 27 — $4.02 nationally (AAA Apr 25 print — flat at $4.02, essentially unchanged from $4.022 Apr 24) — CA highest $5.83; OK lowest $3.38 — Diesel ~$5.62/gal — Largest state increases since Feb 27: Utah +51.7%, Idaho +45.7%, Tennessee +45.6%, Mississippi +45.4%, Kentucky +45% (CAP)
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.076/gal nationally (AAA Apr 17) — down from peak $4.16 on Apr 9 on peace-talk optimism but still highest level since August 2022. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. Diesel national average ~$5.43+/gallon. Jet fuel +88% since Feb 27 — Delta warned of $2B additional Q2 fuel costs. Treasury Sec Bessent: 'optimistic that sometime between June 20 and September 20, we can have $3 gas again.' Treasury Apr 16 designated 24+ people, companies, and vessels in the Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani oil smuggling network; halt on 30-day waivers on Iranian/Russian oil. March CPI (Apr 10) confirmed gasoline +21.2% MoM — largest monthly spike since CPI was first published in 1967. Headline CPI 3.3% YoY. UMich April consumer sentiment 47.6 all-time low. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf taunted: 'Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / Treasury / Quinnipiac / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: +286 pts / +4.16% from pre-war — SATURDAY APR 25: U.S. EQUITY MARKETS CLOSED. Holding at Friday Apr 24 ALL-TIME RECORD CLOSE S&P 7,165.08 (+0.80%); Nasdaq RECORD 24,836.60 (+1.63%); Dow 49,230.71 (-0.16%); Russell 2000 2,787.00 (+0.43%); VIX 18.71 — Next print Mon Apr 27 — DO NOT generate a Saturday tape — Defense ETF ITA +66% trailing 12M, +8% YTD
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 17 RECORD CLOSE: 7,126.06 (+1.20%, +84.78 pts). Dow 49,447.43 (+1.79%, +868.71). Nasdaq RECORD: 24,468.48 (+1.52%, +365.78). Russell 2000 2,776.90 (+2.11%). VIX 17.48 (-2.56%). 10Y 4.24% (-6.8 bps). DXY 97.73 (-0.26%). Gold futures $4,879.60/oz (+1.48%). Silver $81.97 (+4.14%). Bitcoin ~$76,038-$78,000 (+0.55 to +4.5%). Week: S&P +3.6%. Defense mildly rebounded: ITA closed $233.68 (+2.03%), 1-yr total return +55.93%. Energy (XLE) extended sharp losses begun Apr 8 — Benzinga est XLE constituents face ~$76B annualized revenue hit. Airlines ripped higher: Alaska Air (ALK) +13.6% led the R1000; Delta (DAL) closed $71.72 (+2.6%) with implied ~$1.5B Q2 pretax fuel tailwind vs. consensus; JETS up double digits WoW. March CPI +3.3% YoY; gas component +21.2% MoM. UMich consumer sentiment 47.6 — all-time low in 74-year series. FactSet: 10% of S&P reported, 88% beating EPS, aggregate 10.8% above consensus; forward P/E 20.9. IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast, warned further escalation 'a close call for global recession.' Fed Funds futures repriced aggressively toward December 2026 cut (prior: on-hold into 2027). Treasury extended Russian seaborne-oil sanctions waiver through May 16. Markets priced a durable ceasefire on Friday — Iran reimposed Hormuz restrictions Saturday morning Apr 18, creating a dangerous Monday gap. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Bloomberg / FactSet / IMF / Federal Reserve / BLS.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$900M/day (~$0.91B) — BLOCKADE DAY 12 — CENTCOM/Navy Times Apr 24 baseline: 34 vessels redirected since Apr 13 (zero breached); Apr 25: no new boardings or seizures; M/V 'Yuri' supertanker halted near Larak Island — 3 U.S. CARRIERS in CENTCOM (Lincoln, Ford, Bush; first time since OIF 2003) — combined >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines — CSIS 'Last Rounds' Apr 24: 13,000+ targets in 39 days; >1,200 Patriots; >1,000 Tomahawks (~10x annual procurement); ~1,100 stealth cruise missiles (NYT); Patriot/THAAD ~50% drawdown, PrSM ~45%
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 50: ~$850M/day — burn rate re-accelerating with blockade operations (16 warships, 10,000+ personnel) despite paused strike ops on Iran front (ceasefire ~240+ hours). Early-war burn rate ran ~$1.88-2.1B/day during the first week. Carrier strike group steady-state alone runs ~$6.5M/day per CNAS. Every $10/bbl of sustained oil elevation adds $1.3B annually to DoD fuel costs. Munitions depletion is the single most acute strategic constraint: 5,197 munitions across 35 types in first 96 hrs ($10-16B replacement bill in 4 days); Tomahawk ~1,000 fired by late March vs pre-war 3,100-4,500 inventory (Payne calculates 5 years of production needed to replenish first 16 days); JASSM/JASSM-ER 135 fired in first 96 hrs, projected full depletion by early May (RUSI); PAC-2/PAC-3 943 rounds fired in 96 hrs (~18 months of combined production), projected exhaustion mid-June (Boeing seeker critical bottleneck); Arrow interceptors projected depleted by end of March; PrSM+ATACMS 320 in first 16 days (~half of combined inventory). Structural single-source choke points at Williams (F107 turbofan), AMPAC Cedar City (ammonium perchlorate), Holston AAP Kingsport (RDX/HMX) cannot be relieved on operational timelines. CENTCOM: 10,000+ Sailors/Marines/Airmen aboard 16 warships (11 DDGs, 3 LHD/LPD/LSD, 1 CVN, 1 LCS) + aircraft enforcing blockade (Hegseth: <10% of total US naval power). USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) publicly credited Apr 17 with turning back a merchant vessel. Naval disposition (USNI Apr 13): Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (Eastern Med, record deployment), Bush (off Namibia, transiting around Africa), Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (CENTCOM AOR). 21 vessels turned back since blockade began (up from 19 Apr 17); zero evaded. Blockade costs Iran ~$400M/day. Trump (Apr 18): blockade stays until 'transaction with Iran is 100% complete.' Hegseth: 'locked and loaded' on Iranian dual-use infrastructure, power generation, and energy industry should ceasefire collapse. Source: Pentagon / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker / FPRI / Payne Institute / RUSI / CNAS.
daily_cost.change: Est. ~$900M/day ongoing (ceasefire posture, blockade enforcement; 3-CSG posture +>200 aircraft +15,000 personnel) — Day 57 indefinite ceasefire D4 / D18 overall — no new accrual disclosed Apr 25 — FY2027 DoD budget request: $1.5T (+42%) — NYT Apr 24: stockpile depletion at decade-plus pace — AEI/McCusker (NYT): $25B-$35B total — Pentagon-formula extrapolation: ~$51B; Harvard Bilmes: $2B/day, $88B by Day 44; CSIS Cancian: ~$28B; WarCosts.org: ~$35B — wide analyst dispersion

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (57 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$51B midpoint through Day 57 (range $28B-$95B) — held at $51B midpoint pending ceasefire-period accrual; no new disclosure Apr 25 — VERY WIDE analyst dispersion: AEI/McCusker (NYT Apr 24) $25B-$35B; CSIS Cancian ~$28B; WarCosts.org ~$35B; Harvard Kennedy School (Bilmes) $88B by Day 44 — CSIS 'Last Rounds' Apr 24: 13,000+ targets in 39 days; Patriot/THAAD ~50% drawdown, PrSM ~45% — Senate WPR #5 defeated 46-51 Apr 22 — May 1 War Powers deadline (T-6 days)
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Internal site tally: ~$55.8B (Day 50). External estimates triangulate a direct US military expenditure of roughly $50-52B: CSIS reported $3.7B for the first 100 hours; Pentagon told Senate $11.3B for the first 6 days; $16.5B by Day 12; $18B by March 19; Penn Wharton Budget Model $35-38B direct military by April 4, projecting $47B through end-April before blockade operations began. Including indirect US economic losses, PWBM's Kent Smetters projects total cost could reach $210B. Arab country cost exceeds $120B (March 31); Iran's government assesses its own economic damage at $300B to $1 trillion. Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' including long-term costs. Pentagon requested >$200B supplemental from White House (WaPo Mar 18); Deputy SecDef Feinberg leading a parallel ~$50B supplemental workstream; Pentagon comptroller Hurst told Congress eventual request would be 'north of $50B.' Nothing has cleared yet. Senate rejected 4th Iran War Powers Resolution 52-47 Apr 15 (Rand Paul crossing, Fetterman voting nay, Justice absent); House failed its version 213-214 Apr 16 — single-vote margin. Sen. Kaine will force weekly votes through the War Powers Act 60-day deadline, which expires May 1. Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) circulating draft Iran AUMF. FISA Section 702 reauthorization expired Apr 20 after postponed House procedural vote, creating a secondary intelligence-authority crisis. IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast, warned further escalation 'a close call for global recession.' Russia collecting $45-151B additional oil-revenue windfall per KSE Institute. Anti-war protest coalitions continue domestic rallies. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / WaPo / Penn Wharton / Harvard Bilmes / House / Senate / IMF / KSE Institute.
total_cost.change: ~$51B midpoint US military cost through Day 57 — wide range $28B-$95B depending on methodology — Pentagon $11.3B through Day 6 authoritative anchor + ~$1B/day × 50 ≈ $51B — Penn Wharton up to $210B total incl. economic spillovers — Harvard Bilmes (Apr 14): 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' incl. long-term costs — Treasury Apr 24 sanctioning of multiple Iran-tied wallets, ~$344M in cryptocurrency frozen — UN Apr 23: 30M+ globally pushed into poverty even if war ends tomorrow

targets_struck.label: Targets struck (Operation Epic Fury)
targets_struck.note: CSIS Apr 24 'Last Rounds' confirms 13,000+ targets struck over 39 days through ceasefire — INDEFINITE CEASEFIRE D4 / D18 OVERALL HOLDS — no new strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 — Bushehr NPP at 21 consecutive days no new incident — 3 U.S. CARRIERS in CENTCOM (Lincoln + Ford + Bush; first since OIF 2003); combined >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines — 34 vessels redirected by U.S. blockade since Apr 13 — Patriot/THAAD ~50% drawdown, PrSM ~45% (CSIS); >1,200 Patriots, ~1,100 stealth cruise missiles, >1,000 Tomahawks (~10x annual procurement; NYT Apr 24)
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. Cumulative totals as of April 18 per JINSA and White House releases: more than 2,000 command-and-control strikes against Iran, 8,000+ Iranian military targets decimated, Iran's navy reduced from 158+ vessels (per Trump claim) to residual fast attack craft, 97% of Iran's naval mines destroyed or tracked, 70% of Iranian space-launch facilities degraded, and Iran's air force reduced from 30-100 daily sorties pre-war to zero today. US aircraft losses: 39 airframes across 13,000+ sorties — 24 MQ-9 Reapers, 4 F-15E Strike Eagles (3 lost to friendly fire from a Kuwaiti F/A-18), 1 A-10, 1 E-3G Sentry destroyed, 1 F-35A damaged. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~240+ hours, ceasefire holds). Hegseth's Apr 16 posture statement remains operative: US is 'reloading with more power than ever before' and 'locked and loaded' on Iranian dual-use infrastructure, power generation, and energy industry should the ceasefire collapse. Chairman Caine confirmed military objectives have been achieved. The operational mission set has shifted from combat to coercive economic strangulation. Apr 18: Iran reimposed Hormuz restrictions at ~05:19 ET; IRGC gunboats fired on Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald; ~20 outbound tankers reversed course. Lebanon truce breached by killing of French UNIFIL CWO (first allied fatality on Lebanon front). DM Katz: Lebanon campaign 'not yet completed.' Trump Apr 18: barred Israel from further Lebanon strikes. Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28; Iran retains 'thousands of missiles' and one-way attack drones. Source: CENTCOM / JINSA / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / ACLED / AFP / DoD.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 2 non-combat deaths (DCAS) — 400 WIA — no new US casualties Apr 25 (zero new since Apr 7 8 PM ET ceasefire) — 6 KIA Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1), 1 KIA Prince Sultan AB (Mar 8), 6 KIA KC-135 crash western Iraq (Mar 12) — Senate defeated Baldwin WPR 46-51 Apr 22 (5th failed attempt; Sen. Wicker hearing scheduled 'sometime in May' i.e. post-deadline) — 60-day War Powers deadline May 1 (T-6 days)
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM/Pentagon consolidated picture: 13 killed in hostile action + 2 non-combat deaths, 140-520+ WIA depending on methodology. The six Port Shuaiba (Kuwait) KIA from the March 1 drone strike and six KC-135 crew from the March 12 Iraq crash remain the largest clusters. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7, ~240+ hours). ALLIED FATALITY APR 18: one French chief warrant officer serving with UNIFIL was killed and three French peacekeepers wounded in southern Lebanon — first allied fatality on Lebanon front; Macron: 'everything suggests that responsibility for this attack lies with Hezbollah.' Senior US military official told House Armed Services Committee Apr 16 that Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28, and warned Iran retains 'thousands of missiles' and one-way attack drones. Blockade surface force (CENTCOM): 10,000+ Sailors/Marines/Airmen aboard 16 warships (11 DDGs, 3 LHD/LPD/LSD, 1 CVN, 1 LCS) enforcing (Hegseth: <10% of total US naval power); 21 vessels turned back cumulatively. USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) publicly credited Apr 17 with turning back a merchant vessel. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / HASC / Élysée / French MoD.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: DCAS reports 400 WIA — DISCREPANCY: The Intercept (Apr 22) alleges Pentagon scrubbed 15 WIA from DCAS records, calling it 'the definition of a cover-up' — official figure remains 400 with footnote — casualty reporting frozen on Iran front since Apr 7 ceasefire; no confirmed new US combat wounded Apr 8-Apr 25 — congressional officials told MS NOW base damage 'vastly more' than publicly reported — 60-day War Powers statutory deadline May 1
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. Consolidated picture: 140-520+ WIA depending on methodology (CENTCOM public figures vs Intercept/DCAS internal). Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire on Iran front in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~240+ hours). No confirmed new US WIA since ceasefire. Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28. Apr 18: 1 French chief warrant officer with UNIFIL killed, 3 French peacekeepers wounded in southern Lebanon — first allied fatality on Lebanon front. Blockade enforcement: 10,000+ Sailors/Marines/Airmen aboard 16 warships + aircraft; 21 vessels turned back since blockade began; zero evaded. Iran reimposed Hormuz restrictions Apr 18 at ~05:19 ET; IRGC gunboats fired on Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald. Trump Apr 18: 'no sticking points left'; blockade stays until 'transaction with Iran is 100% complete.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / HASC / The Intercept / Élysée.

iranian_killed.label: Iranians killed (identified)
iranian_killed.note: Iran Legal Medicine Organization (Forensic Medicine), Apr 12 figure — 3,375 IDENTIFIED (2,875 men / 496 women); NBC Apr 24 cited 'nearly 3,400' killed since Feb 28 — Hengaw separately estimated 5,300+ Iranian military killed (Mar 18 figure) — no new Iranian Health Ministry print Apr 25 — reporting on Iran front frozen since Apr 7 ceasefire; no new kinetic deaths since then — Russian ambassador (Apr 24): Russia hopes to resume work on completing Bushehr Units 2 & 3 — Pezeshkian Apr 25: 'destroyed our infrastructure' — display attribution prominently
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — reporting frozen by Iran MoHME since Apr 7. Apr 17-18 consolidated picture: MoHME 2,076 killed, 240 women, 212 children, 26,500+ injured (including 1,621 children) — last public update Apr 10, unofficial plateau during ceasefire. Iran forensic chief (Euronews Apr 14): 'at least 3,000 killed.' HRANA (Apr 7): 3,636 documented deaths including 1,701 civilians (254 children) and 1,221 military. Hengaw (military-only, Apr 8): 6,620 Iranian military killed. IDF intelligence (mid-March): 6,000+ IRGC killed, ~15,000 wounded. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. Displacement: ~3.2 million IDPs per UNHCR/Iranian government (800,000 households, 75% from Tehran), with 68,000+ Iranians having crossed into Türkiye. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7 (~240+ hours). Deputy FM Khatibzadeh (Antalya) told AP Apr 18 Iran is 'not ready' for new face-to-face talks — US 'has not abandoned their maximalist position.' Confirmed Iran still holds 440 kg (970 lb) of enriched uranium and called the idea of transferring it out of the country 'a non-starter' — directly contradicting Trump's Friday claim that Tehran had agreed to do so. Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, in his first public message since his Feb 28 elevation, used the Iranian Army anniversary to declare the navy 'ready to inflict bitter defeats on its enemies.' Iran's refusal to allow inspectors since February means the 440 kg HEU stockpile is unverified. Bushehr NPP: Apr 4 strike killed one Iranian protection officer (75m from perimeter); reactor remains undamaged, no off-site radiation at any site. Natanz shows two impact holes above underground halls with 'localized contamination and chemical hazards.' Fordow took direct hits with underground damage that 'can't be determined with certainty' per Grossi. Infrastructure: 25 Iranian hospitals damaged / 9 out of service (NPR); 481 Iranian schools damaged (USCRI); 130+ historical/cultural sites damaged incl. Golestan Palace and Isfahan's Naqsh-e Jahan Square (UNESCO). Iranian rial collapsed to ~1,544,000 IRR/USD (-42% YoY). Source: Iranian MoHME / forensic chief (Euronews) / HRANA / Hengaw / IDF / IAEA / UNHCR / USCRI / NPR / AP.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese killed
lebanese_killed.note: Lebanese Public Ministry of Health — running total 2,495 K (Apr 24 baseline 2,491 + Apr 25 additions; subject to MoPH consolidation) / 7,719 W — Apr 25: +4 K Israeli strikes on a truck and a motorbike in Yohmor al-Shaqeef (Nabatieh district); IDF intercepted a 'suspicious aerial target' in southern Lebanon — Apr 24 violations: IDF killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in Bint Jbeil + 2 civilians in Touline; Hezbollah shot down Israeli drone, attacked IDF APC in Ramyah — Israel-Lebanon CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 3 WEEKS (Trump-brokered Apr 23) but Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad: extension 'meaningless' — UN Human Rights Office (Apr 24): attacks on civilians in Lebanon/Israel 'may amount to serious violations of IHL'
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative Apr 17 (Lebanese MoPH via WAFA/Al Jazeera): 2,294 killed, 7,544 wounded, ≥172 children, 100+ paramedics/health workers dead — material increase from prior 2,167-2,196 range reflecting debris-recovery updates. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday / Operation Eternal Darkness' alone killed 357 and wounded 1,150+. Hezbollah losses: internal sources 400+ fighters, IDF claims 1,400+. Lebanon displacement: 1.2M+, 140,000 in collective sites, 189,000 (mostly Syrians) fled to Syria; 15% of Lebanon under Israeli forced-displacement orders. LEBANON TRUCE BREACHED APR 18 (first major breach of 48-hour-old 10-day ceasefire): French chief warrant officer serving with UNIFIL killed, three French peacekeepers wounded in southern Lebanon — first allied fatality on Lebanon front; Macron: 'everything suggests that responsibility for this attack lies with Hezbollah.' One Lebanese killed in Israeli strike on a motorcycle in the south; earlier MoPH reporting documented one death in Kounine overnight Apr 17. Netanyahu declared the anti-Hezbollah campaign 'not over' but Trump publicly told Israel it is 'prohibited' from striking Lebanon — 'enough is enough.' Lebanese President Aoun declared Lebanon will no longer be 'an arena for others' wars.' Battle of Bint Jbeil consolidated: Brig. Gen. Guy Levy's 98th Division Egoz Unit commandos raided Hezbollah 'combat compound' in Aynata casbah, seized weapons + demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute; 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed cumulatively. WHO: 61+ verified attacks on healthcare in Lebanon since March 2 (~2.9/day); 91+ medical workers killed. OCHA Lebanon Flash Appeal $308.3M through May. UNHCR: 202,477+ cross-border flows into Syria since Mar 2. UNICEF reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. VP Vance reportedly internal administration broker of the Lebanon truce — publicly stated 'ball is in Iran's court.' Source: Lebanese MoPH/WAFA / Élysée / French MoD / IDF / WHO / UNHCR / OCHA / UNICEF / AP.

displaced.label: Displaced (Iran)
displaced.note: Up to 3.2 million displaced inside Iran per UNHCR estimate (Ayaki Ito Mar 12 baseline) — 1.2-1.3M Lebanese internally displaced (OCHA/IRC) — UN Apr 23 (UNDP head De Croo): 30M+ globally pushed back into poverty even if war ended tomorrow (0.5-0.8% global GDP lost) — Pezeshkian Apr 25 urged Iranians to halve home lighting ('two lights instead of ten') signaling continued grid damage — WFP Apr 24 joint report: acute food insecurity 'alarmingly high' in Afghanistan, Bangladesh, DRC, Myanmar, Nigeria, Pakistan, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Yemen
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Regional total: 4 million+. Iran: ~3.2 million IDPs per UNHCR/Iranian government (800,000 households, 75% from Tehran), with 68,000+ Iranians having crossed into Türkiye. 4.4M Afghans in Iran at elevated risk; 70,000+ returns to Afghanistan by mid-March. Lebanon: 1.2M+ displaced (~20% of population), 140,000 in collective sites, 189,000 (mostly Syrians) fled to Syria; 15% of Lebanon under Israeli forced-displacement orders. UNHCR reports 202,477+ cross-border flows from Lebanon into Syria since March 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals). UNICEF's Rapid Response Mechanism in Lebanon has reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. OCHA Lebanon flash appeal seeks $308.3M through May; UNHCR's Iran appeal seeks $36.2M for 1.65M refugees plus host communities. WHO Flash Appeal: $30M for 5 most-affected countries. WHO Lebanon: 60+ tonnes of medical supplies mobilized since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge. ICRC made the first cross-border shipment into Iran from Türkiye on Apr 14 (trauma kits). IASC: 8 aid workers killed in Iran, 5 in Lebanon, 14 in OPT this year. Four Iran border crossings remain open for aid. Lebanon ceasefire (effective 5:00 PM ET Apr 16) breached by French UNIFIL CWO killing Apr 18. Tens of thousands of Lebanese IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite IDF spokesman Adraee's warning not to return south of the Litani yet. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / IOM / UNICEF / IFRC / ICRC / IASC.

flights_cancelled.label: Tehran IKIA international flights resumed
flights_cancelled.note: STATUS CHANGE Apr 25: First IKIA international departures in 56 days — Istanbul, Muscat, and Medina took off Saturday Apr 25; Iran Air domestic Tehran-Mashhad inaugural flight — FlightRadar24 confirmed at least 4 Istanbul departures Saturday morning — Routes to Baku, Najaf, Baghdad, Doha scheduled in coming days — Mehrabad limited domestic ops continuing — Switch dashboard tile from 'cancelled' counter to 'resumed' status indicator (FLIPPED — first resumption since Feb 28); regionwide 52,000+ commercial flights cancelled since Feb 28 remains cumulative but is now superseded by the resumption signal
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. HORMUZ RECLOSED APR 18: Iran Joint Military Command announced via IRIB at ~05:19 ET that strait control 'returned to its previous state ... under strict management and control of the armed forces,' accusing the US of 'piracy and maritime robbery.' Iranian Navy radio instructed merchants that 'no ships are allowed to pass through.' Kinetic incidents Apr 18: (1) Two IRGC gunboats opened fire on Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald carrying Iraqi crude despite prior clearance — ship continued via Iran-sanctioned Larak Island route. (2) Minerva Evropi and Nissos Keros (both Indian-flagged) reversed course after taking fire. (3) Container ship reported rocket damage off Omani coast (UKMTO). (4) ~20 outbound tankers reversed course inside the strait (Kpler/TankerTrackers via WSJ). 'Tehran Tollbooth' mutates: Parliament security committee spokesman Ebrahim Rezaei announced Iran is drafting legislation to replace informal ~$2M/vessel toll with a statutory 'strait security' charge; US and Israeli military vessels barred entirely. NSC head Ebrahim Azizi confirmed ships now require prior Iranian naval authorization. CENTCOM cumulative blockade tally: 21 vessels turned back since blockade began (up from 19 Apr 17); zero have evaded. USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) publicly credited Apr 17 with turning back a merchant vessel. Pre-war Hormuz traffic averaged ~140-178 vessels/day; ~17 vessels transited on reference Saturday Apr 11; first VLCC cleared the strait Apr 14. PARIS SUMMIT APR 17: Macron + Starmer co-hosted 49 countries (many by video, incl. China, India, Canada, Australia, South Korea, Ukraine) for Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative — US pointedly NOT invited. ~12 nations pledged naval assets; Germany (Merz) committed mine-clearance conditioned on UNSC legal basis; Italy (Meloni) offered Italian naval units. Polymarket: Hormuz-reopen-by-EoM odds collapsed from 64% to ~32% on Apr 18 reclosure. War-risk insurance mid-April 0.8-2.5% of hull value per Gulf transit (down from March peak 5% but still 8-20x pre-war). JPMorgan: ~329 vessels in the Gulf with ~$352B insured exposure. IEA Director Birol Apr 16: Europe has 'maybe six weeks or so of jet fuel left' — 'largest oil supply shock in history.' Blockade costing Iran ~$400M/day. Source: CENTCOM / UKMTO / Kpler / WSJ / JPMorgan / IEA / Élysée / Polymarket.

children_killed.label: Children killed (Iran)
children_killed.note: HRANA cumulative — held at 207; no new HRANA print Apr 25 — broader regional total (376 Iranian + 172 Lebanese + 4 Israeli + 1 Kuwaiti + 13 Iraqi ≈ 566) tracked separately — UNICEF: services for 10M affected incl. 2.2M children via damaged health facilities; 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since Mar 2 — 442 health facilities and 763 schools damaged in Iran — UN Human Rights Office (Apr 24): patterns of attacks on civilians in populated areas 'may amount to serious violations of IHL'
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran MoHME reporting frozen since Apr 7 — last public figures: 212 children killed, 1,621 among wounded (Red Crescent Apr 10). HRANA: 254 children among 1,701 Iranian civilian deaths. Lebanon MoPH Apr 17: ≥172 children killed cumulatively; UNICEF reports 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since March 2. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' toll: 357 killed including children. Regionwide, UNICEF/USCRI estimate ~340 children killed and 2,000+ children injured. Iran: 481 Iranian schools damaged (USCRI); 65+ destroyed; 30+ universities struck. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Karaj B1 bridge double-tap (Apr 2) killed 8 and wounded 95+. Pope Leo XIV is on Day 6 of an 11-day Africa tour (April 13-23) and was NOT in Rome on Apr 18 — he celebrated a farewell Mass at Yaoundé-Ville Airport, Cameroon, then departed for Luanda, Angola (arriving ~14:00 GMT). His Apr 17 message from Douala denounced a world 'being ravaged by a handful of tyrants,' and Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally issued a solidarity statement backing his 'courageous call for peace.' Peace vigil operations in Rome continue absent the Pope's physical presence; his return is Apr 23. Trump-Pope feud continued from earlier in the week. Anti-war coalitions (50501, ANSWER, Code Pink, Palestinian Youth Movement, NIAC) continue domestic rallies. Source: UNICEF / USCRI / Iranian MoHME / HRANA / Lebanese MoPH / WHO / HRW / Vatican / Hengaw / NBC.
